exercises.title: Exercise 1
exercises.introduction_context: In order to perform more efficient operations on heap, they are often transformed into static tables. This transformation exploits a breadth-first scan of the heap and puts the results of this scan into the static table.

Question.question_text: Give the necessary declaration of a heap whose nodes contain a single piece of information: integer type key.
Give the necessary declaration of the static array which will be the result of this transformation.
What is the condition on the size of this table?
Question.Solution: condition: size (T) >= number of nodes in the heap

Question.question_text: Write the algorithm: which takes as input the root of the heap and returns: the array containing the elements of the heap as well as the number of elements inserted in the constructed array.
Solution: Procedure Heaps2Array(node : Pointer(TNode); var T : array[1..N] of integer; var l : integer);

exercises.title: Exercise 2
exercises.introduction_context: In the following questions, we assume the existence of standard operations defined on an integer queue Q_i as follows: QueuInit_i which initializes the queue, Enqueue_i (e: integer) which inserts the element e into the queue, Dqueue_i which scrolls (deletes) the existing element at the head of the queue, Queue_Empty_i: Bool which tests if the queue is empty, ValHead_i: integer, which accesses the head element, and Val_Tail_i: integer which accesses the tail element. We also assume the existence of standard operations on a stack of S_i as follows: StackInit_i; which initializes stack_i, Push_i(e:integer) which pushes the elemente in the stack, Puop_i which pops the top element, StackEmpty_i:Bool which tests if the stack is empty, ValTop_i: integer, which accesses the top element.

Question.question_text: • Let P1 and P2 be two stacks. Propose a recursive procedure which superimposes thestack P2 on P1 (i.e., which puts all element of P2 on P1, keeping the order of the elements in the two stacks). The procedure should use only the standard stack operations already mentioned. If temporary stacks are to be used then the minimum possible number of these stacks named Pj(j>2) must be used. Finally, use this signature Procedure ConcatStack

Question.introduction_context: 1. In the following, the requested algorithms can only use the standard operations already mentioned and therefore do not have access to the internal representation of the queue (the pointers)
Solution: if (m < ValHead1) then m ← ValHead1 ; EndIf ;

Question.question_text: By using only the standard operations...
Consider the balanced binary A tree...

Solution: if (ValHead0 -> LC <> nil) then Enqueue0(ValHead0 -> LC); EndIf;
if (ValHead0 -> RC <> nil) then Enqueue0(ValHead0 -> RC); EndIf;

exercises.title: Exercise 3
exercises.introduction_context: You are given a Binary Search Tree (BST) in which exactly two nodes have been swapped by mistake. This corruption breaks the BST property. Your task is to restore the BST without changing its structure, by only swapping back the two incorrect node values.

Question.question_text: Input: A pointer to the root of a BST where two nodes are swapped. Output: The BST should be corrected so that it becomes a valid BST again.
Question.example_text: Example (in-order traversal shown): Original BST (correct): 1 3 4 6 7 8 10. Corrupted BST (two swapped): 1 3 10 6 7 8 4.
Question.Solution: Correction not provided in the text.
